text:
Market today: Risk-on mood led by Meta's massive 10% pop on its AI cloud push - that's the real driver today. AI infrastructure names are catching a bid, which directly benefits your chip/tech concentration. But the CoreWeave/Nebius selloff shows the market is already rotating who wins in AI cloud, so not all boats rise.

Your holdings: SOXL (-11.09%) is your biggest concern - it's a 3x leveraged chip ETF that decays daily and your loss is already deep; stop is at $200, watch it closely on margin. SPAL (-12.36%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged IPO - down hard with a tight stop at $21.38, nearly triggered; margin amplifies this pain fast. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec down sharply with stop at $9.50 - smallest cushion in the book.

Watch: 1) Whether Meta's cloud push accelerates or kills AI infrastructure names like AMAT/SMH. 2) SPAL's $21.38 stop - it's close.

Bottom line: Your leveraged losers need active stop management today - one gap and margin makes it ugly.
plays: SPAL: Stop at $21.38 is nearly touched right now - confirm it's live and hard, not mental. On margin, a leveraged ETF breaking that level can cascade fast.
SOXL: Down 11% and leveraged 3x - if it breaks $200 stop, cut it without debate. Decay plus margin is a slow bleed that turns into a fast one.

No new buys today - Meta's move is real but the scanner setups (AMAT entry ~$659, ALAB entry ~$444) are in premium/chasing territory with no fresh catalyst beyond today's noise. Wait for a clean pullback.
